record.field: EMQX is a high-performance, distributed MQTT broker for connecting, processing and routing massive volumes of IoT and real-time messaging data.

EMQX is a high-performance, distributed MQTT broker for connecting, processing and routing massive volumes of IoT and real-time messaging data.

Written in Erlang/OTP, EMQX implements MQTT 5.0, 3.1.1 and 3.1 (plus MQTT over QUIC, MQTT-SN, CoAP and LwM2M) and is built to handle millions of concurrent device connections with low latency. It pairs raw connectivity with a built-in SQL rule engine and dozens of data integrations, making it a unified data backbone for IoT, IIoT, connected vehicles and telemetry platforms rather than just a message relay. A single self-hosted node runs in production for free with all features enabled.

Key features
  • Full MQTT 5.0 / 3.1.1 support, plus MQTT over QUIC, MQTT-SN, CoAP and LwM2M gateways
  • Massively scalable: millions of concurrent connections on a masterless, clustered architecture
  • SQL-based rule engine for filtering, transforming and routing messages in real time
  • Data integrations ("bridges") to Kafka, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, cloud services and webhooks
  • Enterprise-grade security: TLS/mTLS, X.509, JWT, LDAP and PSK authentication with fine-grained ACLs
  • Web dashboard, REST API and Prometheus/Grafana observability for operations
Best for

IoT and industrial IoT platforms, connected-vehicle and telemetry backends, and any team that needs a reliable, observable MQTT broker capable of scaling from a single self-hosted node to large multi-node deployments.

services: emqx: image: emqx/emqx-enterprise:6.0.1 hostname: node1.emqx.com environment: EMQX_NODE_NAME: emqx@node1.emqx.com expose: - 1883 - 8083 - 18083 volumes: - ./emqx_data:/opt/emqx/data - ./emqx_log:/opt/emqx/log networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network restart: unless-stopped healthcheck: test: - CMD - /opt/emqx/bin/emqx_ctl - status interval: 30s timeout: 5s retries: 3 labels: - traefik.tcp.routers.emqx-mqtts.entrypoints=mqtts - traefik.tcp.routers.emqx-mqtts.rule=HostSNI(`broker.yourdomain.com`) - traefik.tcp.routers.emqx-mqtts.tls.certresolver=letsencrypt - traefik.tcp.routers.emqx-mqtts.service=emqx-service - traefik.tcp.services.emqx-service.loadbalancer.server.port=1883 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:18083' networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
